title: NICHOLAS REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Nicholas reaction describing Lewis- or Brønsted-acid-promoted decomplexation and substitution chemistry of dicobalt hexacarbonyl-stabilized propargylic alcohol complexes.
notes: Key concepts: formation of Co2(CO)6-alkyne complexes, generation of cobalt-stabilized propargylic cations, nucleophilic trapping, oxidative/reductive decomplexation, and access to substituted alkynes or enynes.

title: NICHOLAS REACTION
summary: Application page for the Nicholas reaction featuring the beta-lactam precursor to thienamycin, (+)-secosyrins 1, (+)-epoxydictymene, and 5-methylene-7-phenyl-ethynyl-oxocane assembled through intramolecular or tandem Nicholas sequences.
notes: Examples emphasize intramolecular Nicholas cyclizations, controlled Lewis-acid-mediated decomplexation, and use of cobalt-alkyne complexes in complex natural-product synthesis.

title: NOYORI ASYMMETRIC HYDROGENATION
summary: Overview page for Noyori asymmetric hydrogenation describing BINAP-Ru-catalyzed enantioselective hydrogenation of functionalized olefins and carbonyl compounds to chiral acids, alcohols, and amines.
notes: Key concepts: BINAP-Ru catalysts, H2 pressure, high enantioselectivity for beta-keto esters and enones, dynamic kinetic resolution, and predictable stereochemical outcome with functionalized substrates.

title: NOYORI ASYMMETRIC HYDROGENATION
summary: Application page for Noyori asymmetric hydrogenation featuring (-)-haliclonadiamine, the CD spiroketal unit of spongistatin 1, and (-)-morphine prepared through highly enantioselective Ru-catalyzed hydrogenation or transfer hydrogenation steps.
notes: Examples emphasize beta-keto ester reduction, allylic alcohol hydrogenation, and Ru-mediated asymmetric transfer hydrogenation in complex alkaloid synthesis.

title: NOZAKI-HIYAMA-KISHI REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Nozaki-Hiyama-Kishi reaction describing CrCl2-mediated coupling of allyl, vinyl, aryl, or alkynyl halides with aldehydes or ketones to form allylic or homoallylic alcohols.
notes: Key concepts: organochromium formation from organic halides under CrCl2, Ni-catalyzed variants, chemoselective carbonyl addition, stereocontrol in macrocyclizations, and broad utility in fragment coupling.

title: NOZAKI-HIYAMA-KISHI REACTION
summary: Application page for the Nozaki-Hiyama-Kishi reaction featuring deacetoxycalyconin acetate, the C1-C19 fragment of (-)-mycalolide, (-)-aspinolide B, and the C12-C13 trisubstituted olefin portion of epothilone D assembled by NHK coupling.
notes: Examples emphasize intramolecular NHK macrocyclization, fragment coupling of vinyl iodides with aldehydes, and use of CrCl2/NiCl2 systems in complex natural-product synthesis.
